description_long: This triumphal arch is constructed from large, rectangular stone blocks arranged in a regular pattern, giving it a robust and ancient appearance. The archway itself is a single, wide opening with a rounded top, framed by the same stonework as the rest of the structure. The surface of the stones is rough and uneven, with visible texture and color variation, ranging from dark gray to lighter gray hues. The top of the arch is flat and broad, providing a solid capstone effect. There are no visible decorative reliefs, sculptures, or inscription panels, making the design simple and utilitarian. The overall form is reminiscent of Roman architectural styles, emphasizing strength and durability.
description: A robust stone triumphal arch with a single rounded opening, built from large, textured gray blocks and lacking decorative elements.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide, rounded archway and the regular stone block pattern.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thickness of the structure and the continuous stone texture.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with the same archway and stone block arrangement.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the structure's depth and stonework.
